query: evals are green on the kestrel-ops plugin — package it into a marketplace and write the TESTING.md so the ops team can install it
the meridian-claims plugin passed all its evals. how do we actually get this onto the adjusters' machines? they'll need install steps and somewhere to learn the commands
Set up the team-facing site for the plugin — overview page, install guide, and a tutorials wiki with one article per phase command.
we're shipping harborview-underwriting next week. put together the rollout kit: pilot workshop outline, support channel, and the demo-video handoff list
trigger accuracy hit the target this morning, so testing is done — publish the plugin and stand up the wiki for the data team
need marketplace.json written next to the plugins dir — the catalog bundles the vendor snowflake plugin too, so it has to be multi-plugin
regenerate .build/deploy.json and the dashboard so the champion has one stable link before thursday's pilot kickoff
before the pilot starts, get the misfire channel announced — add it to TESTING.md and the site's support notes so teammates know where to report wrong-skill fires
The plugin is built and the evals pass. Run the final validation sweep and get it packaged for release — no client names or machine paths can leak into what the team installs.
can you seed the tutorials wiki from the graded practice-run transcripts? each phase command needs a worked example written in the team's own vocabulary
run the trigger benchmark on the built plugin and tell me whether hold-out accuracy clears the bar before we even think about shipping
a teammate on the intake pod says the plugin fired the wrong skill for her quote request — here's her message, turn it into an eval case and a fix
catalog's approved for the kestrel project — scaffold the plugin and build the pilot subset of skills first
Here are the champion's dashboard notes on the built skills: rename /quote-fast, merge the two intake skills, cut the reporting one. Apply them and re-verify.
digests are done for the atlas-supply project. what skills and commands should the team plugin actually have? draft the catalog for approval
deploy the staging branch of our claims api to the k8s cluster and roll it back automatically if the health checks fail
spin up a docs site for our internal python sdk — install instructions, tutorials, and an api reference
how should I word a skill description so it wins triggering against a sibling skill? want the doctrine's take before I touch it
set up a synthetic practice project and run the plugin end to end on it so we know it works before anyone else touches it
the plugin's been live for a month — do a health pass over the feedback/ directory and pin any misfires as eval cases
